key_drivers: The policy backdrop is still defined by a neutral Fed versus tighter non-U.S. central-bank signals, with the RBA having hiked in March and the BoJ still in gradual normalization mode.
Energy and safety premia remain elevated: official EIA data showed a crude draw, while Strait of Hormuz disruption risk has re-escalated and is keeping the oil supply backdrop tight.
U.S. growth is softer rather than collapsing. GDPNow slipped to 1.2%, credit spreads widened, and the curve flattened, leaving broad index beta mixed even as SOX remains firm.
Gold and silver are being pulled in opposite directions by slower growth and elevated geopolitical risk on one hand versus March gold-ETF outflows and a firmer intraday dollar tone on the other.

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 and HY OAS reflect normal publication lag relative to the report timestamp.
World Gold Council ETF-flow data is the April 8 publication covering March 2026 flows.
EIA inventory data is the latest official weekly report for the week ended April 10, 2026 pending the next release on April 22.
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used to classify DXY, SOX, MOVE, and copper direction, including copper because the preferred Investing.com page was blocked in this environment.
The official ECB policy decision page was used as the fallback source for eurozone growth after Reuters and S&P Global PMI pages were inaccessible.
An accessible Al Jazeera report was used to verify renewed Strait of Hormuz disruption risk after other current-news pages were restricted.
overnight_changes: DXY was modestly firmer on the day but still softer over one month, so the score was kept flat rather than strongly USD-negative.
VIX remained in the balanced 15-20 band while rising on the day, keeping risk regime neutral but short-term equity volatility less friendly.
SOX stayed firmly higher, whereas MOVE and credit spreads moved the wrong way for broad beta and small caps.

imminent: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report due April 22, 2026
Ongoing U.S.-Iran negotiations around the Strait of Hormuz ceasefire window
near_term: Next Atlanta Fed GDPNow update on April 29, 2026
background: ECB hawkish-hold versus softer euro-area growth
BoJ normalization remains supportive for JPY on divergence grounds
